title: Is a happy ending incompatible with Eva
display_title: Is a happy ending incompatible with Eva
wikitext:
[]: Is a happy ending "incompatible" with Eva?             Quote Post by chaosakita » Wed Nov 18, 2009 3:25 pm Lots of people here say that there is absolutely no way that a Evangelion series could have a happy ending. But I really have to wonder why. What is it about Evangelion that is inherently upsetting? Now, the original series was very depressing (what a understatement). But in the end, I view it to be very optimistic anyways. Depending on one's view of it, the ending can be still be a happy one, even if there was a lot of trauma involved. But I don't think just because bad things happen, a story is automatically pessimistic. Now, this was all written when Anno had some psychiatric issues and was probably using the series for self-counseling. A similar work considered great that was also written by a depressed author is Watchmen by Alan Moore. It was considered revolutionarily "dark" for the day, and helped usher in an "edgier" era of comics. But despite its wide acclaim, Moore just described it as something he wrote while he was under a "bad mood." So if Moore had not been depressed, he would not have written something like that, and his newer works have been sort of lighter in tone. Anno was depressed in 1996. Now flash forward more than 10 years later, and after therapy, he feels better. Is there really anything to say that, just like Moore, Anno's writing won't reflect his mood? Perhaps Anno wants to troll with audience of Japan once more. But I think if he does want to produce a "serious" work once more, it will end up happy. NGE was already an optimistic story; it wouldn't take too much to stretch those themes into producing a happy story. By happy, I don't necessarily mean the Angelic Days manga where everything is so nicely wrapped up in a sanitized manner (but I wouldn't mind). But by expanding on the current themes of optimism that are already present in Rebuild (in even greater number than NGE!), it's not too hard to see a world where the characters can understand each other a little bit more. I think it's interesting to note that in the years following NGE, Anno also wrote part of the screenplay of Cutie Honey. Is that the sign of someone who wants to write a depressing story again? So in short: The original NGE was sad because the director is sad, and the director isn't anymore...

[]:             Quote Post by Natsuka_Chie » Wed Nov 18, 2009 3:33 pm I'm not sure if it will be a happy or a bad ending (but I'd like to hove one like: well.. we're pretty messed up, but there's still hope, and all that). Besides that, I think the ending will teach us something, like the whole NGE did. edit: oh and I don't thing NGE had a sad ending.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Wed Nov 18, 2009 3:38 pm You may be wanting this thread -- http://forum.evageeks.org/viewtopic.php?t=7389 -- unless the topic is going to stay well distinct. Watchmen very much reflected the Zeitgeist, as the US and USSR were in the final stages of the staring match known as the Cold War. And at the end, Moore blinked, feeling unable to leave us with the better world that emerged from the turmoil. In that respect, Promethea , while it returns to the Earth, at least retains the benefit of the transformative moment. Anno's original depression was more from the inside; making his return to the series something else.

[]:             Quote Post by chaosakita » Wed Nov 18, 2009 3:43 pm I still think Moore's "bad mood" had a lot to do with the darkness of Watchmen. He says it himself: http://io9.com/5162215/what-is-watchmen The apocalyptic bleakness of comics over the past 15 years sometimes seems odd to me, because it's like that was a bad mood that I was in 15 years ago. It was the 1980s, we'd got this insane right-wing voter fear running the country, and I was in a bad mood, politically and socially and in most other ways. But it was a genuine bad mood, and it was mine .

[]:             Quote Post by Lucretius » Wed Nov 18, 2009 3:50 pm I'm fairly certain that Rebuild will end on an optimistic note for Shinji--but not necessarily for any of the other characters. I can't see Rei living happily ever after as a normal human, given her otherwordly nature.

[]:             Quote Post by Joseph the PRPD » Wed Nov 18, 2009 3:58 pm I don't have a problem with Rebuild actually having a happy ending. I have my hopes up for it.

[]:             Quote Post by chee » Wed Nov 18, 2009 4:00 pm No, it's not incompatible at all. It's about despair AND hope, not just despair. (However, it seems like a lot of fans only look skin-deep, and see only the dark aspects, and gain a shallow impression of what NGE "should" be.)

[]:             Quote Post by Merridian » Wed Nov 18, 2009 4:09 pm chaosakita wrote: By happy, I don't necessarily mean the Angelic Days manga where everything is so nicely wrapped up in a sanitized manner (but I wouldn't mind). But by expanding on the current themes of optimism that are already present in Rebuild (in even greater number than NGE!), it's not too hard to see a world where the characters can understand each other a little bit more.

[]:             Quote Post by MatrixRefugee » Wed Nov 18, 2009 5:07 pm I have to admit that I find Angelic Days weirdly endearing: it's no replacement for the core of the mythos, but it's a sweet cheerful place to visit once in a while. On the other hand, I wind up yawning when people seem to get tunnel vision about the level of despair in the series; I've grappled with depression for much of my life, and since I know too well how it feels, it's something I'd like to see every sufferer of it -- even fictional ones -- get a respite from, or better still, manage to get a handle on. I'm hoping Rebuild has a more upbeat ending and I'd like to see something similar happen when Sadamoto's manga is completed. As I've said elsewhere, everyone (even Gendo) deserves a better ending this time around (Well, except the SEELE dudes...). Personally, I find the TV ending more despairing than the ending of EoE: EoE gave us the promise that people could return from Instrumentality and rebuild the world -- perhaps a little more wisely this time, but the TV ending seemed to suggest that people could just as likely withdraw into an inner world of their own making, which wasn't the real reality. Might be something I've learned from the Matrix fandom, that it's likely better to embrace reality, with all it's sorrows, than to live in a false reality. Not that it's a wrong way to exist, it just may not be healthy, or you may not be as whole a person as you could be if you did.

[]:             Quote Post by Lucretius » Wed Nov 18, 2009 5:56 pm MatrixRefugee wrote: I have to admit that I find Angelic Days weirdly endearing: it's no replacement for the core of the mythos, but it's a sweet cheerful place to visit once in a while.

[]:             Quote Post by chaosakita » Wed Nov 18, 2009 6:29 pm Let's look at some people (one person) at TvTrope's view of Rebuild that led me to make this topic: Now with 70% less angst!... seriously.

[]:             Quote Post by Lucretius » Wed Nov 18, 2009 8:59 pm I didn't think the tone of the movie was really a radical departure from the original series. Good things happened to Shinji in olde NGE too. And the final scene wasn't entirely hopeful. Sure, Shinji takes decisive action, but it's nearly at the cost of an apocalypse, and it seems to be just what Gendo wants. The manga seemed like it might have had a purely happy ending too, but now it's going the EoE route. If Rebuild ends happily, it won't be unmitigated happiness.

[]:             Quote Post by Rodeo » Wed Nov 18, 2009 9:09 pm The only thing that's incompatible with Eva is an ending without instrumentality. It might be hard to make the aftermath of an apocalypse seem all cheerful.

[]:             Quote Post by schismatics » Wed Nov 18, 2009 9:10 pm chee wrote: No, it's not incompatible at all. It's about despair AND hope, not just despair. (However, it seems like a lot of fans only look skin-deep, and see only the dark aspects, and gain a shallow impression of what NGE "should" be.)

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Wed Nov 18, 2009 10:01 pm The original series had TWO happy endings.

[]:             Quote Post by chaosakita » Wed Nov 18, 2009 10:15 pm Rodeo wrote: The only thing that's incompatible with Eva is an ending without instrumentality. It might be hard to make the aftermath of an apocalypse seem all cheerful.

[]:             Quote Post by jayngfet » Wed Nov 18, 2009 11:07 pm I think it'll have death, it'll end with some characters crushed, but I'm hoping for instrumentality to be averted. Shinji however is doomed no matter what when you think about it, his life revolved around the eva and he never really had much of a life before it. It defines a signifigant part of his personality. At the end either his EVA is cast away and he loses that part of himself, or it's used to kill humans and it takes a toll on him again. Even if no one dies and everybody gets there emotions out and they no longer need the EVA's it'll be hard. They're no longer needed, they no longer needed. Lots of things would need to be tied up, whatever happens to the Rei clones is likley not wholly pleasant if they all live(imagine having to live with exact copies of you running around, knowing you were never meant to be). Outside the geofront there'd be lots to deal with as well, SEELE still out there controlling everything, the UN is a militaristic force, and hey, they still need to rebuild the rest of earth and figure out how to get life on earth back to it's feet. It isn't going to be happily ever after no matter what happens, not by a long shot.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Wed Nov 18, 2009 11:57 pm MatrixRefugee wrote: I've grappled with depression for much of my life, and since I know too well how it feels, it's something I'd like to see every sufferer of it -- even fictional ones -- get a respite from, or better still... etc

[]:             Quote Post by Kanchan » Thu Nov 19, 2009 12:16 am I don't think anyone minds if it ends in happy ending. But I think people are specifically disappointed in how 2.0 was heading to the typical anime direction...the part where he pulls Rei out of angel is just is your simple anime 101... pretty gay. TV Evangelion started out like a typical anime and turned in to something utterly different and unique. People want to be shocked ... so far Ano is giving us Kaorwu that is keeping us on the edge as he seems to be more integrated....

[]:             Quote Post by EvaCub » Thu Nov 19, 2009 12:46 am Kanchan wrote: the part where he pulls Rei out of angel is just is your simple anime 101... pretty gay.
